commentaries.author: Gregory The Dialogist
commentaries.content: 29. Behold how in relating the true order of his creation, he suddenly bursts forth into the pride of haughty arrogance, and, in other words, repeats the same statement.

commentaries.author: Julian of Eclanum
commentaries.content: [Elihu] believes that with the aid of a comparison he can make some effective assertions against Job. “If you,” he says, “cannot reply to me, a creation of God, be certain that you will be much less able to reply to God, the Maker of everything.” - "Exposition on the Book of Job 33.4–5"
